---
title: "Framework-Agnostic Positioning"
description: "Understand why Aionis works across HTTP, SDK, MCP, Codex, and LangGraph-style runtimes instead of belonging to one framework."
---

# Framework-Agnostic Positioning

Aionis does not belong to one framework.

The failure mode it solves is broader than any single orchestration stack.

## Why Framework-Agnostic Matters

Boundary failure happens in:

1. coding agents
2. planner-executor pipelines
3. MCP-based local workflows
4. graph-style agent runtimes

If Aionis is described too narrowly, adoption shrinks to one ecosystem.

## What Aionis Is Across Stacks

Aionis works as:

1. an execution memory layer
2. a continuity layer
3. a replay and policy layer

that can integrate through:

1. HTTP
2. SDKs
3. MCP
4. framework-specific adapters and workflows

## Why This Is Credible

The current repository already exposes:

1. API routes
2. TypeScript and Python SDKs
3. MCP servers
4. Codex local workflow scripts
5. LangGraph smoke coverage

This is already reflected in the implementation surface.
